name: main.py
observations: CLI entry point — coeus binary via pyproject.toml scripts
Registers 8 subcommands: ingest, ask, search, stats, watch, config, job, setup
Each command constructs its own ZvecStore+Embedder+Oracle stack — no shared singleton
Provider selection: if embedding_model startswith 'voyage' use VoyageAIEmbedder else OpenRouterEmbedder
cmd_ask calls expand_chunk on every returned chunk to add ±3 line context before printing

name: mcp_server.py
observations: MCP server entry point — coeus-mcp binary via pyproject.toml scripts
At module load: monkeypatches builtins.print to write to stderr; redirects stdout fd to stderr fd; installs OutputDetector on sys.stdout to catch any remaining leaks
Oracle initialized in daemon thread at module import; first tool call blocks up to 30s on _oracle_init_event
coeus_query tool hardcodes project=None — always searches all projects, no project filter possible
coeus_reinit_oracle resets global state and waits 10s (not 30s) — may return before init completes
SSE mode: restores real stdout before mcp.run(); wraps uvicorn with HostRewriteMiddleware to fix Docker host header
Oracle.ask() serialized via threading.Lock — concurrent MCP calls queue behind each other
Supports both stdio (default) and SSE (--sse flag) transports

name: config.py
observations: Singleton pattern via module-level _config + get_config(); reset_config() sets it to None
Config is a frozen dataclass — immutable after construction
Reads ~/.coeus/.env via python-dotenv at every from_env() call
COEUS_DATA env var controls data_dir; db_path is always data_dir/coeus.db
is_valid() only checks VOYAGE_API_KEY when model is voyage-3 — does not validate OpenRouter key
chunk_overlap default 100 chars, chunk_size default 1000 chars — both configurable via env

name: core/oracle.py
observations: Oracle is the retrieval orchestrator
Constructor warms FastPath from entities table via _warmup_fastpath — exceptions silently ignored
ask() is protected by threading.Lock — non-reentrant
FastPath exact match (confidence=1.0) or prefix match (confidence=0.9) short-circuits all vector/FTS work if confidence>0.9
Retrieval: search_hybrid(limit*4) then search_fts(limit*2) separately — FTS called twice total (also inside search_hybrid)
Dedup by 'source:start_line' composite key — same content at same location from different methods collapses to one
Temporal keywords trigger 1.2x score boost: latest,recent,new,update,changed,today,yesterday,last,current,status,v0.,v1.,v2.
Chunk threshold: top_score*0.3; pointer threshold: top_score*0.05 — dynamic, not absolute
entities field in QueryResult always contains first 5 DB entities ordered by insert — not query-filtered
savings dict tracks potential_tokens and actual_tokens but is never used by callers (not written to ledger)

name: core/ingestor.py
observations: Ingestor owns chunk->embed->store pipeline
Skip-if-unchanged: compares mtime float equality against files table — sub-second changes on coarse filesystems miss
File read: UTF-8 first, latin-1 fallback — no binary detection
_choose_chunker: tries AST first (catches all exceptions silently), falls back to line-count
delete_documents_by_source called before every re-ingest — deletes documents AND entities for that source
doc_id = {stem}_{index}_{md5[:8]} — collision possible if same stem in different dirs with same content
Embed batch size is unlimited — all chunks for a file sent in one embedder.embed() call
Extractor runs on full raw text for every file regardless of chunking method
ingest_directory skips: hidden path parts, node_modules, __pycache__, venv, .git, dist, build

name: core/fastpath.py
observations: In-memory only — rebuilt from entities table on every Oracle init, not persisted
PrefixTrie._collect() is recursive DFS — no depth limit, deep tries risk stack overflow
Trie nodes cap at 10 documents per node — older entries may be evicted silently on index()
FastPath.index() only adds to exact_index if len(key)<100 or '@' in key — long entity content skips exact match
Prefix match requires doc.content.lower().startswith(query_lower) — trie hit + content-start check both required
Returns None if no match — Oracle proceeds to vector+FTS path

name: core/extractor.py
observations: Pure regex extraction — no NLP, no ML
Extracts: problems, solutions, decisions, tasks (checkbox), code_blocks
Decision extraction: line-by-line state machine; captures valid_from/valid_to date ranges in [YYYY-MM-DD -> YYYY-MM-DD] format
Decisions that reach EOF without a blank line or bullet separator are still captured (flush at end of text)
code_snippets captured but NOT stored as entities — only problems/solutions/decisions/tasks go to DB
has_data() returns False if only code_snippets found

name: core/contextualizer.py
observations: expand_chunk reads source file from disk on every call — no caching
Adds ±3 lines of context around the stored start_line/end_line range
Returns None silently if file does not exist or read fails — caller falls back to stored content
Line calculation: lo = max(0, start_line-1-3), hi = min(len(lines), end_line+3)

name: core/ast_chunker.py
observations: Supports .py .go .js .ts .tsx .rs via tree-sitter grammars
_LANGUAGE_CACHE dict caches grammar objects and None values — missing grammar cached as None forever for process lifetime
Grammar loading: ImportError or AttributeError silently cached as None — no warning logged
.tsx uses language_tsx() function; all others use language()
Packs consecutive small segments into chunks up to chunk_size bytes — greedy left-to-right
Oversized single nodes (larger than chunk_size) produce one chunk larger than chunk_size — never split
AstChunk.section stores the function/class name for the last segment packed into the chunk
Returns empty list if no top-level nodes found — Ingestor then falls back to line-count chunker

name: core/setup.py
observations: detect_tools checks existence of ~/.claude, ~/.cursor, ~/.continue, ~/.codeium/windsurf, ~/.config/opencode
register_claude_code writes SKILL.md to ~/.claude/skills/coeus/SKILL.md and registers mcporter config
_merge_json deep-merges at one level: nested dicts are updated, non-dict values are replaced
register_vscode_continue requires pyyaml — raises ImportError if missing
CLAUDE_CODE_SKILL_CONTENT contains HyDE instructions: rephrase conceptual queries as hypothetical answers before calling coeus_query
write_env_file strips known keys (VOYAGE_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, COEUS_EMBED_MODEL) from existing .env before rewriting

name: core/job_manager.py
observations: JobManager is not auto-started by CLI or MCP — must explicitly call start_worker()
handle_ingest is an async function but _process_job calls asyncio.run() — fails if existing event loop running
Worker progress denominator hard-coded to 10: progress=int((files_processed/10)*100) — caps at 100% after 10 files
Worker polls storage.list_jobs with status='pending' every 1s sleep
Jobs are stored in ZvecStore jobs table — shared SQLite DB

name: storage/zvec_store.py
observations: Single SQLite connection with check_same_thread=False — not fully thread-safe for concurrent writes
SQLite PRAGMAs: WAL journal mode, NORMAL synchronous
Vector store: separate directory ~/.coeus/vectors/docs/ using zvec library (HNSW)
Vector dimension read from settings table on reopen — constructor param ignored if DB exists
insert_documents: commits SQLite first, then inserts to zvec — partial failure leaves SQLite committed but zvec missing vectors
delete_documents_by_source: deletes SQLite rows and entities, then deletes zvec vectors one-by-one by id
search_vector: fetches limit*4*5 from zvec then post-filters by project via SQL — project filter not in vector index
search_fts: wraps each word in double-quotes — AND match of exact quoted terms, no stemming, no fuzzy
search_hybrid: RRF with k=60 combining vector and FTS ranked lists
FTS5 virtual table maintained by SQL triggers on INSERT/DELETE — no trigger for UPDATE (documents has no UPDATE path)
get_entities() does not filter by valid_to — all entities returned including logically expired ones
get_active_decisions() filters by valid_from/valid_to only when date param provided — no default date filter
ledger table populated only by record_savings() — Oracle.ask() tracks savings in QueryResult.savings dict but never calls record_savings()

name: storage/interface.py
observations: Document: id(str), content, embedding(optional), metadata(optional dict), source(str), project, start_line, end_line
Entity: id(optional int auto), type, content, file_path, project, valid_from, valid_to, superseded_by
SearchResult: document, score(float), method(str) — method can be 'vector', 'fts', 'hybrid', 'fastpath', or compound like 'vector+fts'
StorageInterface ABC defines the contract — ZvecStore is only concrete implementation

name: embedders.py
observations: VoyageAIEmbedder: batch_size=128; dimensions: voyage-3=1024, voyage-3-lite=512, voyage-large-2=1536
OpenRouterEmbedder: no batching — sends all texts in one HTTP request with 60s timeout
create_embedder: provider detection is string prefix check, not enum — typos in provider string raise ValueError
EmbedderInterface alias for Embedder — legacy name

name: watcher/watcher.py
observations: Requires watchdog — raises ImportError at Watcher() construction if not installed
Debounce: threading.Timer reset on each file event; flushes batch after debounce_interval seconds of silence
Tracks only .py .md .js .ts .tsx .json .txt — same as ingest default extensions
Filters out hidden path components and directories
Watcher.run() blocks in while True: time.sleep(1) — designed for foreground use only
No file deletion events handled — deleted files remain indexed until explicit re-ingest or manual cleanup

name: ZvecStore._init_vector_store
observations: Opens existing zvec store at vectors/docs/ if directory exists, creates new one otherwise
On open: reads vector_dimension from settings table; constructor param ignored
On create: uses constructor param, saves to settings table
No dimension validation on open path — mismatch with embedder is undetected until search_vector raises ValueError

name: Oracle.ask
observations: Signature: ask(query, project=None, limit=10) -> QueryResult
limit parameter controls vector/FTS candidate counts (multiplied internally) but also final chunk/pointer slice [:limit]
FastPath bypass: if confidence>0.9, returns QueryResult with single chunk, empty pointers, empty entities
Hybrid search: calls storage.search_hybrid(query_embedding, query_text, limit*4, project) — oversamples 4x
Additional FTS: calls storage.search_fts(query, limit*2, project) — in addition to what hybrid already ran
Rank candidates sorted descending by boosted_score
chunk_threshold = top_score*0.3 — relative, not absolute
final slices: chunks[:limit], pointers[:limit], entities[:5]

name: Ingestor.ingest_file
observations: Returns dict: {chunks: int, entities: int, status: 'indexed'|'unchanged'}
status='unchanged' returned without any storage interaction when mtime matches
Deletes all prior documents and entities for source before re-indexing — atomic replacement
entities count from extracted.count() includes code_snippets in count but code_snippets not stored to DB

name: FastPath.search
observations: Returns None or dict with document, score, confidence, type keys
confidence=1.0 for exact match, 0.9 for prefix match — Oracle checks >0.9 so only exact triggers fast path
Prefix match condition: trie hits AND doc.content.lower().startswith(query_lower)
No fallback to vector search inside FastPath — just returns None
